text: Which exact statistical and noise method governs the strict greater-than-3% comparator?
why_it_matters: D-006 requires a statistically valid comparison but delegates the precise method; it controls false regression and false pass decisions.
unlocks: PERF-FR-002, PERF-FR-004, PERF-FR-007, PERF-FR-010, controlled-runner conformance, and comparator goldens.

options.label: Paired bootstrap median
options.pros: Interleaving reduces drift and the one-sided confidence bound expresses the 3% magnitude rule directly.
options.cons: Requires more runner time and a versioned bootstrap/quantile implementation.

options.label: Mann-Whitney plus ratio
options.pros: Uses an established nonparametric rank test.
options.cons: Significance and magnitude are separate, and the method loses pairing.

options.label: Aggregate mean threshold
options.cons: Too sensitive to noise and outliers for the accepted performance decision.

default: Use 5 warmups, at least 30 alternating valid pairs, 10,000 deterministic paired-bootstrap resamples of median degradation, a one-sided 99% lower bound strictly greater than 3.0%, no sample trimming, zero migration/throttle events, and less than 1.0% benchmark-core background utilization; record the method in a domain ADR before Ready.

answer.text: Option 1: use the paired-bootstrap median method: 5 warmups, at least 30 alternating valid pairs, 10,000 deterministic paired-bootstrap resamples, a one-sided 99% lower bound strictly greater than 3.0%, no sample trimming, zero migration or throttle events, and under 1.0% benchmark-core background utilization.
